title: What are Arrays?
description: Store multiple values in a single variable.

slides.title: Arrays 📚
slides.content:
Arrays are used to store multiple values in a single variable, instead of declaring separate variables for each value.

Think of an array as a list of items written inside square brackets `[]`.

slides.title: Creating an Array
slides.content: To declare an array, define the variable type, specify the name of the array followed by square brackets, and specify the number of elements it should store.
slides.codeSnippet: string cars[4]; // An array that can hold 4 strings

slides.title: Populating Initialization 🛠️
slides.content:
We have declared the array above, but it's empty.

To insert values immediately, you can use a comma-separated list inside curly braces `{}`.

slides.title: Example
slides.content: Here is an array of cars and an array of numbers.

quizData.question: Which of these perfectly creates an array of 5 integers?
quizData.explanation: Type Name[Size] = {values}; is the correct format.
